commentary: Game 2 doctrine: no exit-blocking (they never exit), garrison BOTH 20-VP hexes hard, decoys and occupation economy, bank my own exits late, cavalry doubles as eastern raider/decoy for 1920/2311. GT1: 112/113 seize 0211/0502; 101/102 reinforce 0502's ring, 103/104 back 0211; 105/106 central reserve; Wilder+108 hold 0822 as the southern delay/fly-paper; 110/111 fall back on 1108 ahead of the southern mass; 109 lurks northeast to bait their cavalry east and take 1920/2311 when Maney vacates.

Decoy confirmed: their cavalry chases 109 northeast, away from my west. 109 dodges to 2404, keeping two cavalry brigades busy, with 2311 (20 VP, empty) as the dive target when the gap opens. 110/111/114 form a 13-strength delay stack on 1108. Main body flows to the twin fortresses: 101/102 to the 0502 ring, 103/104 to the 0211 ring, 105/106 central reserve, 112 corrected onto 0211, 113 stands on 0502. Wilder+108 (13 str) hold 0822 for real this time - let their odds-checker refuse it.

109 dives onto 2311 - a 20-VP Union-scoring hex, empty, flips on entry - with three cavalry brigades fly-papered behind it. Wilder pulls onto 0822 with 108 briefly then both will slip NW before the 26-CSP avalanche lands; credit only flips if they physically pass through. 110/111 join 114 on 1108 (13-strength delay stack). 101/102 close the 0502 ring, 103/104 the 0211 ring, 105/106 to central reserve. 147 runs the west-edge escape ahead of Gracie; 148 dodges north out of Clayton's ZOC if the gate allows.

Their cavalry overshot north - 109 has a clear dive to 2311 (20 VP, flips on entry), then 1920 next. 110/111 escape the closing central mass northwest to 0908; Wilder and 108 slip out of the 0822 pocket toward 0516 before 40 CSP finishes ringing them (the credit holds unless they pass through). 148/149 run east along the bottom edge to become the eastern task force with 109 - the whole east is empty of enemies below row 16. 147 closes on the 0502 ring; both fortress rings stand.

The creek explains the cavalry's stall: 2311 is reachable only over the 2411 bridge - 109 swings east to cross properly. 149/151 continue the south-edge run to the east; artillery 150 takes the west-edge route. Wilder/108 fall back on 0516 covering the fortress approach; 110/114 hold the 1108 credit one more turn with 111 in support; both 20-VP rings stand; 101/102 close the last gap at 0503.

109 is pinned on 2311 by three cavalry brigades - if it falls, the hex credit stays Union and the trade is 5 CSP for 20 VP plus six turns of decoy work. Wilder/108 slip the closing southern pocket north to 0417; 147 breaks for 0316 ahead of Bates/Wright; artillery 150 hugs the west edge. The eastern route is closed - 151/152 are pinned at 1128 and future entries go west, always. Center: 110/114 fold to 0705 toward the ring, 105/111 screen at 0908. Both fortress rings stand.

Their whole army chases decoys while both fortresses sit untouched. Wilder/108 and 147 keep the west-edge parade moving north; 153 takes the cleared west route home; 114 folds to 0806. The 0908 screen and the pinned pairs at 1128 and 2311 stand their mandatory ground - every turn they survive pins triple their weight in Confederate strength. Rings unchanged.

109 stands adjacent to Scott on 2311 - its forced attack is a free lottery ticket: a defender-retreat result walks 109 back onto the 20-VP hex by advance. The west-edge parade continues north through the open cols 02-03; 153 and 150 thread the southwest corner away from their artillery. The 0908 rock keeps its ground - two enemy brigades dead there already because mandatory combat makes them feed it. Rings unchanged, still untouched at GT8.

Night moves. The decoy parade turns exit-column: Wilder/108 to 0315, 147 to 0215 - two more marches and they bank 18 VP off the map while 45 CSP of pursuers inherit an empty corner with four turns left. 153 takes a dawn-attack position two hexes from their isolated artillery at 0225 (night forbids entering its ZOC); 150 and 156 continue west-edge routes. 106 deepens the north face at 0406 against the converging Gregg/McNair/cavalry group. The 0908 rock is locked and stands; rings unchanged.

Exit column: Wilder/108 make for 0111, 147 for 0101 - banking 18 VP and stranding their 45-CSP wing in a corner with four turns left. North face reinforces ahead of their 37-CSP concentration: 106 to 0206, 101 to 0304. The southern stragglers pair into the col-02 corridor; artillery 150 tucks into the corner out of counter-battery range. The 0908 rock, the 1128 pins, and both fortress garrisons stand.

Final approach: Wilder/108 to exit 0111, 147 to exit 0101 - one march, then off the map for 18 VP with forty CSP of pursuers stranded behind them. 159 takes the west route home. North face thickens quietly; the rock and the pins hold their mandatory ground; fortress rings unchanged.

The pursuit has claws in the parade - locked units fight their mandatory battles at the west edge; whoever breaks free keeps running for the exits. 157 has fallen at the 1128 pin; 158 carries it alone. Everything else holds: rings, rock, pins.
The parade broke contact - Wilder/108 are three hexes from exit 0111, 147 follows to 0101. Bank the VP; hold everything else to the end.

Wilder and 108 walk off the map - 13 VP banked, and the wing that chased them for ten turns inherits nothing. 147 reaches the exit behind them for the final turn. All garrisons stand for the last night.

Final turn: 147 steps off the map to complete the parade's escape - three units, 18 CSP, exited under the noses of their largest wing. Every surviving garrison stands for the final scoring: both 20-VP fortresses denied, the ring unbroken.
